revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:味鋺神社]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| name = Ajima Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image = File:Ajima-jinja ac (1).jpg
| location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|qid=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Nagoya)|1=北區}}, Kusunoki Ajima 2-736
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.218611|N|136.927778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| ISO = JP-23
| Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|coordinates={{Coord|35|13|6.7|N|136|55|40.3|E|qq=}}|width=200|float=center|border=infobox|caption=|qq=}}{{OSM location map
 | coord = {{Coord|35|13|6.7|N|136|55|40.3|E|qq=}}
 | zoom = 12
 | float = right
 | width = 200
 | height = 200
 | shape1 = image
 | mark1 = Red pog.svg
 | label1 = Ajima Shrine
 | mark-size1 = 5
 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|35|13|6.7|N|136|55|40.3|E|qq=}}
 | label-pos1 = bottom
 | label-size1 = 10
 | label-color1 = black
 |qq=}}
| Deities = Umashimaji no Mikoto, Aji no Migata no Mikoto
| type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
| consecration_year = Unknown
| Main Hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri
| Festivals = October 10
| location = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1207964|1=Kita-ku, Nagoya}}
}}
'''Ajima Shrine''' (味鋺神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|北區|en|北區|qid=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku (Nagoya)|1=北區}}, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture.
== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine enshrines Umashimaji no Mikoto, regarded as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, and is identified with the Ajima Shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as part of Kasugabe District in Owari Province. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was also known as "Rokusho Myōjin."
In 1093 (Kanji 7), equestrian rituals were held, and during the annual festival, {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|qid=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} was traditionally performed, although this ceased after World War II. Yabusame was later revived in 1955 to commemorate Nagoya's municipal merger and was re-enacted with ceremonial costumes in 1998.
The adjacent {{ill|Gokoku-in Temple|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in Temple|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632505}} is believed to have been established as "Yakushi-ji" by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} during the Tempyo era (729–748), with Ajima Shrine serving as its tutelary shrine.
The surrounding area, once known as "Hyakotsuka," contained over 50 tumuli collectively called the Ajima Kofun Cluster, but they were lost to development. In 1978, a stone bridge known as "Kiyomasa Bridge," constructed by {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|qid=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|1=Katō Kiyomasa}} for the {{ill|Inaki Kaidō|ja|稲置街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaki Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaki Kaidō|1=Inagi Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596828}}, was relocated to the shrine grounds. It is speculated that the bridge may have repurposed materials from a kofun stone chamber.
== Deities ==
; Main Deities
* {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇麻志麻治命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウマシマジ}}
* {{ill|Aji no Migata no Mikoto|ja|味饒田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aji no Migata no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aji no Migata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Associated Deities
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* {{ill|Raijin|en|Raijin|qid=Q12808082|lt=Raijin|1=Raijin}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
== Grounds ==
* Main Sanctuary
* Worship Hall
* Chōzuya (Purification Fountain)
* Statue of Ajima’s {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|qid=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}}
* {{ill|Banhira|ja|蕃塀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banhira|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banhira|1=banpei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124160302}} (decorative wall)
* Kiyomasa Bridge
== Gallery ==
<gallery>
Ajima-jinja shaden ac.jpg|Main Sanctuary
Ajima-jinja haiden ac (1).jpg|Worship Hall
Ajima-jinja ac (2).jpg|Ajima's Yabusame Statue
Ajima-jinja banpei ac (1).jpg|{{ill|Banhira|ja|蕃塀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banhira|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banhira|qq=}}
Kiyomasa bridge.jpg|Kiyomasa Bridge
Gokokuin.jpg|Adjacent {{ill|Gokoku-in Temple|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in Temple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in Temple|qq=}}
</gallery>
== Access ==
* {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|qid=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line|1=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} – Approximately 700 meters southwest of {{ill|Ajima Station|en|Ajima Station|qid=Q4699746|lt=Ajima Station|1=Ajima Station}}.
* [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Tokai Transportation Services]] [[:en:Tōkai Transport Service Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] – Approximately 1.3 km south of {{ill|Ajima Station (JR)|ja|味美駅 (JR東海交通事業)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Station (JR)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Station (JR)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ajiyoshi Station (Jōhoku Line)|qid=Q4699834}}.

* {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Futago-chō, Kasugai)|ja|白山神社 (春日井市二子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Futago-chō, Kasugai)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Futago-chō, Kasugai)|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579597)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579597}} – Traditionally relocated from Ajima.
* {{ill|Ajima Kofun Cluster|ja|味美古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Kofun Cluster|1=Ajiyoshi kofun group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417037}}
